title: CIS Benchmarks Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on hardening systems, Level constraints, and automated auditing.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Center for Internet Security (CIS) Benchmarks?

options.text: They provide a legal framework for prosecuting software piracy.
They provide globally recognized, consensus-driven technical guidelines detailing exactly how to harden and secure configurations for OS, Cloud, and Kubernetes environments.
They dictate the color schemes and user interface laws for federal websites.
They replace traditional Anti-Virus software.

questions.question: Why is it generally bad practice to deploy a production application directly on the default, out-of-the-box configuration of an Operating System or Cloud environment?

options.text: Default configurations are notoriously slow and laggy.
Because vendors (like AWS or canonical) explicitly forbid deploying production apps on default settings via their EULA.
Default configurations are almost universally optimized to prioritize user convenience and ease-of-use rather than stringent security.
It is completely fine and recommended to use default configurations for production.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the difference between Level 1 and Level 2 CIS recommendations?

options.text: Level 1 is exclusively for hardware firewalls, Level 2 is for software firewalls.
Level 1 provides practical security enhancements that shouldn't disrupt applications; Level 2 is extremely aggressive security for sensitive environments that can and will break legacy applications.
Level 1 applies only to Windows, Level 2 exclusively applies to Linux distributions.
There is no functional difference; the levels simply categorize them alphabetically.

questions.question: Which of the following would NOT typically be found as a requirement inside the CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark?

options.text: Ensuring IAM password policies require specific characters and length.
Ensuring no Security Groups allow SSH access from 0.0.0.0/0 (the whole internet).
Ensuring that the company mandates a specific dress code for remote workers logging into the AWS console.
Ensuring CloudTrail is actively encrypting logs globally across all regions.

questions.question: In a modern enterprise, how do DevOps teams typically ensure their enormous Kubernetes clusters comply with the massive list of CIS rules continuously?

options.text: By manually reviewing configuration files line-by-line during the monthly meeting.
By asking the developers nicely to remember the rules before committing code.
By executing automated auditing tools like Kube-bench within CI/CD pipelines to instantly generate pass/fail reports against the official benchmarks.
By completely disabling external networking so the cluster cannot be scanned.
